projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

The front yard camera is not transmitting video and is displaying an error message stating that no frames have been received. This is consistent with recent intermittent camera failures observed during the night.

insights.summary: The simultaneous video dropouts in the front yard and dog run cameras, coinciding with a sudden spike in unclassified audio and a doorbell chime at 06:05, suggest a potential visitor or delivery is occurring while visual confirmation is blocked. This prevents verification of an early morning event that may require attention.
[dog_run] The 'dog_run' camera is experiencing a technical failure and is not receiving any video frames.
[front_yard] The front yard camera is offline and not providing any video feed.

The simultaneous video dropouts in the front yard and dog run cameras, coinciding with unclassified audio in those exact zones, strongly indicate a localized network or power fault affecting those specific outdoor cameras rather than an intrusion or wildlife event. Steve should check the outdoor network switch, Wi-Fi extender, or power supply for those zones to restore monitoring.
[front_yard] The camera was temporarily non-functional but has resumed streaming.
[front_yard] The front yard camera is not transmitting video, which is part of an ongoing pattern of intermittent failures during the night.

The simultaneous loss of video from the dog_run and front_yard cameras, while audio sensors in the same zones continue to report unclassified noise, strongly indicates a localized network or PoE power fault affecting the outdoor camera cluster rather than actual outdoor activity. This matters because it blinds the perimeter monitoring and requires physical inspection of the outdoor network switch or breaker.
[dog_run] The 'dog_run' camera is currently non-functional, preventing any visibility into the dog run area.
[front_yard] The front yard camera is currently offline, showing an error message.
[front_yard] The front yard camera is currently down, showing a 'no frames have been received' error.
